Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies MyNir as EVM0012344 in the Abstract, Results, and Methods.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MyNir is explicitly described as NAD(P)H nitrite reductase in the Results and figure text.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for MyNir appears in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: MyNir is a gene from Mycena; the studied fungus is identified as Mycena purpureofusca.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The fungal species is explicitly given as Mycena purpureofusca in the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Functional disruption was introduced into Mycena strain YSWM, but the gene itself was identified from the SHXG genome assembly; assigning the gene specifically to 'strain YSWM' is not cleanly supported.

Applied_Species.reason: The symbiotic germination assays were performed on Gastrodia elata seeds.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Gastrodia elata is explicitly the plant species studied.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MyNir is discussed within nitrogen metabolism / nitrogen assimilation pathways in Mycena.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper specifically places MyNir in nitrate/nitrite assimilation, alongside nitrate reductase and nitrate/nitrite transporter genes.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: MyNir functions in assimilation, but the exact label 'midstream assimilation step' is not used in the paper.

Primary_Substrate.reason: As nitrite reductase, the enzyme is directly linked to nitrite assimilation in the text.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper states Mycena can reduce nitrate to ammonium and identifies MyNir as key to this assimilation process, supporting ammonia/ammonium as product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Reduction of nitrite to ammonia in N assimilation is supported, but the explicit mechanistic wording 'using reducing power from NAD(P)H' is inferred from the enzyme name rather than directly demonstrated.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper supports that MyNir contributes upstream by enabling nitrogen assimilation that supports downstream metabolites and germination rather than directly producing the terminal trait metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show MyNir disruption lowers IAA and impairs nitrogen-related support of seed germination; the paper links MyNir to both IAA-related effects and nitrogen nutrient supply.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IAA is a phytohormone, and assimilated nitrogen/derived amino acids are nutrient metabolites; both classes are consistent with the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Hormones were detected in Mycena cultures and hyphae, and the functional/metabolomic consequences were observed in G. elata seeds/protocorms.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Results support that reduced MyNir lowers tryptophan/tryptamine and IAA and impairs germination, energy-related sugar mobilization, and growth-related processes.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Deletion of MyNir impaired nitrogen utilization, reduced IAA, tryptophan, and tryptamine contents, and reduced seed germination and swelling ratio.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in the paper for MyNir: transcriptomics, correlation analysis, targeted deletion, growth assays, RT-qPCR, hormone/metabolite quantification, germination assay, metabolomics, and TEM.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: MyNir is called a key enzyme, but the stronger claim 'key control step' or 'rate-limiting' is not explicitly established.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not assign MyNir to an 'assimilatory ferredoxin/NAD(P)H-dependent nitrite reductase family' or provide a family/domain classification.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: MyNir was upregulated during symbiosis, notably at 10–30 dac, and positively correlated with seed germination rate.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper mentions MyNr, MyNrt, and MyGdhA in the same pathway/correlation analysis but does not show physical or functional protein-protein interactions with MyNir.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper provides BioProjects PRJNA1297343 for the Mycena genome and PRJNA1303739 for the interactive transcriptome; metabolomics has no accession, so 'NA' is consistent.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Symbiotic co-culture with G. elata at 25°C in the dark and nitrogen-source variation in minimal medium are explicitly described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: ΔMyNir showed impaired growth on nitrogen media and reduced invasion/expansion on SCM medium.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible application extrapolation, but the paper does not directly discuss breeding/engineering deployment value.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly reports IAA biosynthesis was reduced by 37.22%–38.57% in ΔMyNir.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported by the Results: induction/correlation during symbiosis, impaired nitrogen utilization and colonization upon deletion, and reduced tryptophan/tryptamine/IAA with blocked germination and downstream physiological disruption.

corrections.reason: No EC number for MyNir appears in the paper.
The paper does not assign MyNir to an 'assimilatory ferredoxin/NAD(P)H-dependent nitrite reductase family' or provide a family/domain classification.
The paper mentions MyNr, MyNrt, and MyGdhA in the same pathway/correlation analysis but does not show physical or functional protein-protein interactions with MyNir.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Abstract and Methods explicitly identify MyAmid as EVM0010270.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MyAmid is explicitly described as amidase in the Results and Abstract.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for MyAmid appears in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: MyAmid is a Mycena gene, and the fungal species studied is Mycena purpureofusca.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper names the fungus as Mycena purpureofusca.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Knockout work used strain YSWM, but the gene inventory came from the SHXG genome; specifying the source as 'strain YSWM' is not explicitly resolved.

Applied_Species.reason: The target plant in functional assays is Gastrodia elata.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Gastrodia elata is explicitly named throughout.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MyAmid is discussed as part of the tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis pathway in Mycena.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper states Mycena contains indole-3-acetamide-related IAA biosynthetic apparatus proteins and functionally validates amidase, supporting the IAM branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Calling it the 'downstream/terminal conversion step' is biochemically plausible, but the paper does not explicitly define step position.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The indole-3-acetamide branch is implied, but the paper does not explicitly state that MyAmid uses indole-3-acetamide as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: MyAmid is functionally tied to IAA biosynthesis, and its deletion reduces IAA production.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: That amidase hydrolyzes indole-3-acetamide to IAA is consistent with pathway annotation, but the paper does not directly describe the reaction in this exact biochemical wording.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MyAmid is presented as directly involved in IAA biosynthesis, and its deletion decreases IAA content.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: IAA is explicitly the relevant terminal metabolite linked to MyAmid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: IAA is a plant hormone/phytohormone, as reflected in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: IAA was detected in Mycena cultures and hyphae, and functional outcomes were measured in G. elata seeds/protocorms.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show mutant symbiosis reduced auxin-responsive gene expression, altered polysaccharide metabolism, changed ultrastructure, and inhibited germination, supporting these functions of fungal-derived IAA.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper directly shows that MyAmid deletion reduces IAA production and significantly inhibits symbiotic seed germination.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are used for MyAmid in the paper: transcriptomics, correlation analysis, gene deletion, RT-qPCR, HPLC hormone quantification, germination assay, metabolomics, and TEM.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: MyAmid is called a key enzyme, but the paper does not prove it is rate-limiting or uniquely controlling flux.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: No amidase family/domain assignment is provided in the paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Amidase genes were upregulated during symbiosis, and MyAmid genes positively correlated with seed width-to-length ratio.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MyALDH is mentioned as another correlated IAA-biosynthesis gene, but no interaction with MyAmid is shown.

Omics_Data.reason: The cited BioProjects for genome and transcriptome match the Data Availability section; no metabolomics accession is given.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Symbiotic co-culture with G. elata seeds at 25°C in darkness is explicitly described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Deletion reduced IAA production and reduced invasion/expansion on SCM, indicating poorer symbiotic performance.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference and is not directly stated by the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly notes multiple amidase genes were upregulated/correlated, and MyAmid EVM0010270 was selected as a representative key enzyme for validation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary point is supported: upregulation/correlation during symbiosis, 61.33%–69.28% IAA reduction in mutants, and suppression of germination with altered auxin signaling, polysaccharide metabolism, and ultrastructure.

corrections.reason: No EC number for MyAmid appears in the paper.
No amidase family/domain assignment is provided in the paper.
MyALDH is mentioned as another correlated IAA-biosynthesis gene, but no interaction with MyAmid is shown.
